landing.badge: Free to use · No card required
landing.heroTitle: Every subscription. Every policy. One calm overview.
landing.heroSubtitle: Stop losing track of what you pay for — and what protects you. Hugo gives you a single, honest overview of every recurring charge and insurance policy.
landing.featuresTitle: Everything you need, nothing you don't
landing.featuresSubtitle: Built for people who want clarity, not complexity.
landing.featureSmartImportDesc: Connect Gmail or Outlook and let AI detect your subscriptions from receipts and billing emails automatically.
landing.featureInsuranceDesc: Track all your policies, upload documents, and let AI find coverage gaps and overlaps.
landing.featureRenewalDesc: Get notified before subscriptions renew or trials convert to paid. No more surprise charges.
landing.featureAnalyticsDesc: See exactly where your money goes with category breakdowns, trends, and monthly comparisons.
landing.featureLanguageDesc: Full support for both languages. Switch freely between Danish and English at any time.
landing.featureCancelDesc: When you remove a subscription, Hugo shows you a direct link to the service's cancellation page.
landing.ctaStorySubtitle: Hugo was built by a parent who wanted a clearer view of the family's finances. Named after their child, it's a reminder that financial clarity isn't just about money — it's about the people it protects.
landing.emailImportSubtitle: Connect Gmail or Outlook and let AI scan your receipts and billing emails. In seconds it detects your recurring subscriptions — you review and approve. No manual entry needed.
landing.gmailDesc: Connect your Google account. Hugo searches your last 6 months of Gmail for billing-related emails and surfaces your active subscriptions.
landing.outlookDesc: Connect your Microsoft account. Hugo searches your Outlook, Hotmail, or Live inbox for receipts and billing emails to detect recurring charges.
landing.privacyReadOnly: Read-only access — we never send or delete emails
landing.privacyAI: AI parsing — no human ever sees your inbox
landing.howItWorksSubtitle: Three steps. No integrations. No bank access required.
landing.step1Desc: Sign up with just your email. Free, no credit card.
landing.step2Desc: Enter each subscription or insurance policy — name, amount, renewal date.
landing.step3Desc: Your dashboard shows combined totals, coverage insights, and upcoming renewals.
landing.ctaSubtitle: It takes less than two minutes to get started.

pricing.title: Free. Completely.
pricing.subtitle: Hugo is free to use with no hidden tiers, no feature paywalls, and no expiry. Every feature — subscriptions and insurance — is available to every user from day one.
pricing.whyFreeBody: Hugo was built out of frustration with losing track of recurring charges — not as a business model. Keeping it free removes the barrier to actually using it. If Hugo ever introduces a paid tier in the future, all existing features will remain free.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Straight answers to the questions we get asked most.
faq.happyToHelp: We're happy to help — reach out any time.
faq.a1: Yes. Hugo is completely free with no hidden tiers, no feature limits, and no expiry. Every feature — including Gmail import, Outlook import, insurance tracking, household sharing, and email reminders — is available to all users at no cost.
faq.q2: Do I need to connect my email?
faq.a2: No. Email import is optional. You can add every subscription manually in under a minute each. The Gmail and Outlook integrations exist to save time — they're never required.
faq.q3: What happens to my emails when I run an import?
faq.a3: Your emails are read into memory, parsed by AI, and then immediately discarded. Email content is never written to our database or logs. The only thing saved is the list of subscriptions you explicitly approve. You can read the full explanation on the Gmail or Outlook integration pages.
faq.a4: Gmail (Google accounts) and Outlook (Microsoft accounts — including Hotmail and Live). Both use read-only OAuth access, so Hugo can never send, delete, or modify any email.
faq.q5: Does Hugo need access to my bank account?
faq.a5: No. Hugo never connects to any bank or financial institution. You enter subscription amounts manually, or import them from billing emails. That's it.
faq.q6: How accurate is the AI import?
faq.a6: It's good at spotting clear billing emails — receipts, invoices, renewal notices — but not perfect. It may miss subscriptions that don't send formatted receipts, or occasionally suggest something that isn't a recurring charge. That's why every result goes through a review step where you approve or reject each item before anything is saved.
faq.a7: Hugo supports any currency. Each subscription can be tracked in its original currency, and your dashboard converts everything to a single display currency using live exchange rates. You can set your preferred display currency in your account settings.
faq.q8: Can I share subscriptions with my partner or family?
faq.a8: Yes. Hugo has household sharing — one person creates a household, invites others by email, and shared subscriptions and insurance policies appear in everyone's dashboard. Each member can also have their own private items that stay separate.
faq.q9: Will I get notified before a subscription renews?
faq.a9: Yes. Hugo sends email reminders 7 days before any subscription renewal date. If a subscription has a trial end date, you'll also get an alert 3 days before the trial converts to paid. Reminders can be turned off in account settings.
faq.q10: How does price change detection work?
faq.a10: When you run a Gmail or Outlook import scan, Hugo compares the detected amounts against your existing subscriptions. If the name matches but the price is different, it flags it as a potential price change — showing old vs new price — so you can decide whether to accept the update.
faq.a11: Yes. You can permanently delete your account and all associated data from the dashboard settings at any time. Deletion is immediate and irreversible — there is no recovery period.
faq.a12: Passwords are hashed with bcrypt before storage. OAuth tokens (Gmail/Outlook) are stored encrypted and scoped to read-only access. We use HTTPS for all connections. Email content is never persisted — it's read into memory, processed, and discarded.
faq.a13: Hugo Insurance is a module that lets you track all your insurance policies in one place — health, car, home, life, travel, and more. You can see your total monthly and annual premiums, upload policy documents, and get smart insights about your coverage.
faq.q14: How does overlap and gap detection work?
faq.a14: Hugo automatically analyzes your active insurance policies to find potential issues. If you have two or more policies of the same type (e.g. two home insurance policies), it flags a possible overlap. If you're missing commonly recommended coverage types like health, home, car, or liability insurance, it suggests you consider adding them.

about.title: Built to keep your finances honest.
about.intro: Subscriptions and insurance are designed to be forgotten. Small monthly charges that never feel urgent enough to cancel, policies you haven't reviewed in years — until you add them all up. Hugo exists to make that total impossible to ignore.
about.p1: Most people have no idea what they spend on recurring services and insurance each month. Not because they're careless — but because services make it easy to sign up and hard to keep track. A $9.99 subscription here, overlapping insurance policies there, a family plan that costs more than you remember agreeing to.
about.p2: Hugo is a single place to see everything. Track your subscriptions manually or import them from Gmail and Outlook. Add your insurance policies and let Hugo detect coverage gaps and overlaps. From there, you see what you actually spend each month, what's renewing soon, and where your money goes.
about.p3: No bank connection required. No syncing your financial accounts. No black box that decides what counts. Just a clean, honest view of your subscriptions and insurance — updated when you say so.
about.value1Body: A dashboard that tells you what you spend in two seconds is worth more than a hundred charts. Hugo is deliberately simple.
about.value2Body: Your email is scanned in memory and discarded. Nothing is logged. Nothing is sold. We store the minimum needed and nothing more.
about.value3Body: Hugo is free because subscription tracking shouldn't cost money. There are no paywalled features, no usage limits, and no expiry.
about.ctaTitle: Ready to see your full picture?
about.ctaSubtitle: It takes less than two minutes. No card required.

gmail.title: How Gmail import works
gmail.subtitle: Hugo can scan your Gmail receipts to detect recurring subscriptions automatically. Here's exactly what happens — and what we never touch.
gmail.step1Body: When you click Connect Gmail, you're taken to Google's own sign-in and consent screen. You decide whether to allow access. Hugo requests only the minimum permission required: read-only access to your Gmail messages. We cannot send, delete, or modify anything.
gmail.step2Body: Once connected, clicking Import from Gmail triggers a search of your last 6 months of email for messages with billing-related keywords in the subject — things like receipt, invoice, subscription, renewal, and payment. We fetch a maximum of 50 matching emails.
gmail.step3Title: AI reads and parses the emails
gmail.step3Body: The sender, subject, date, and a short excerpt of each email are sent to an AI model in a single request. The AI identifies recurring subscription charges — service name, amount, billing cycle, and next renewal date. No human at Hugo ever sees your emails. The raw email content is never written to disk or a database.
gmail.step4Title: You review and choose what to import
gmail.step4Body: The AI's suggestions appear in a review panel. Every item is pre-selected, but you can uncheck anything you don't want. Only the subscriptions you explicitly approve are saved to your Hugo account — as ordinary subscription records, identical to ones you'd add manually.
gmail.step5Body: You can revoke Hugo's access to your Gmail at any time by clicking the Gmail button in the dashboard header and choosing Disconnect. This immediately deletes your stored OAuth tokens from our database. You can also revoke access directly from your Google Account at myaccount.google.com/permissions.
gmail.dataTableTitle: What we store — and what we don't
gmail.dataTableSubtitle: Plain answers to what happens to your data.
gmail.row1Why: Needed to call the Gmail API on your behalf when you trigger a scan.
gmail.row2Why: Read into memory during the scan, then discarded immediately.
gmail.row3Why: Only a short excerpt is passed to the AI. Never written to our database.
gmail.row4Why: Shown in the review panel only. Discarded when you close the modal.
gmail.row5Why: Saved as normal subscription records in your account — same as adding manually.
gmail.privacyBody: Your inbox is private. Hugo reads the minimum necessary to detect subscriptions — and nothing more. Email content is never logged, never stored, and never shared with anyone except the AI service used for parsing. You are always in control: the import only runs when you trigger it, and you approve every subscription before it is added to your account.
gmail.ctaSubtitle: Create a free account and connect Gmail in under two minutes.
